concepts.title: Definition and an example of both implicit and explicit functions
concepts.conceptBody:
Explicit Function 

If $\text{x}$ and $\text{y}$ are two variables connected by a relation such that $\text{y}$ is expressed explicitly in

terms of $x$ or $x$ is expressed explicitly in terms of $y$, i.e., $y=f\left( x \right)$ or $f\left( x \right)=y$. Such

functions are known as explicit functions.

For examples $\text{y}=\text{x}+2,\text{xy}+\text{y}-5=0,{{\text{x}}^{2}}+{{\text{y}}^{2}}=5$ are explicit functions.

Implicit Function 

If the variables $\text{x}$ and $\text{y}$ are connected by a relation such that neither $\text{y}$ is expressed

explicitly as a function of $\text{x}$ nor $\text{x}$ is expressed explicitly as a function of $\text{y}$. Such functions are

known as implicit functions. These functions are expressed in the form

$f\left( x,y \right)=0$

For example, ${{x}^{3}}+{{y}^{3}}+3\text{axy}=0,\text{tan}\left( {{x}^{2}}+{{y}^{2}} \right)+\text{cos}\left( x+y \right)={{e}^{x}}$ are implicit functions, of $x,y$.

concepts.title: Transformations done to draw the graph of y=f(x)+a or y=f(x)-a from the known graph of y=f(x)
concepts.conceptBody:
Transformation of graphs

Transformation 1

Drawing the graph of $y=f\left( x \right)\pm a$, from the graph of $y=f\left( x \right)$

(a) To draw the graph of $y=f\left( x \right)+a$,

shift the graph of $y=f\left( x \right)$, a units in upward direction.

(b) To draw the graph of $y=f\left( x \right)-a$

shift the graph of $y=f\left( x \right)$, units in downward direction.

Logic : The graph can be taken as

$\text{y}\pm \text{a}=\text{f}\left( \text{x} \right)$, so we are just changing the value of $\text{y}$ here.

Q) Plot the following : 

(a) $y=\left| x \right|-2$ (b) $y=\text{si}{{\text{n}}^{-1}}x-1$

Solution : 

(a) $\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }y=\left| x \right|-2$

we know the graph of $y=\left| x \right|$ (i.e. modulus function) and the given function can be

written as $\text{y}+2=\left| \text{x} \right|$ also.

applying the transformation for $\text{y}=\text{f}\left( \text{x} \right)+\text{a}$

shift the curve of $y=\left| x \right|$ by 2 unit downward.

(b) $y={{\sin }^{-1}}x-1$

$\begin{align} & \text{or we can write }y+1={{\sin }^{-1}}x \\ & \text{put } y+1\to y \\ & \text{now,} y={{\sin }^{-1}}x \\ \end{align}$

Q) Plot the following : 

(a) $y=\text{sin}x+5$ (b) $y=\text{co}{{\text{s}}^{2}}x$ 

Solution : 

(a) $y=\text{sin}x+5$

$\begin{array}{*{35}{r}} {} & \text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ writing }\!\!~\!\!\text{ it }\!\!~\!\!\text{ as }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }y-5=\text{sin}x \\ {} & \text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ applying }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ y}-5\to \text{y} \\ {} & ~\Rightarrow \text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ y}=\text{sinx} \\ \end{array}$

(b) $y=\text{co}{{\text{s}}^{2}}x$

we don’t know the graph of $\text{co}{{\text{s}}^{2}}\text{x}$ but we do know the graph of $\text{cosx}$.

since $\text{cos}2\text{x}=2\text{co}{{\text{s}}^{2}}\text{x}-1$

$\Rightarrow \text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }2\text{co}{{\text{s}}^{2}}\text{x}=\text{cos}2\text{x}+1$

$\Rightarrow \text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ co}{{\text{s}}^{2}}\text{x}=\frac{\text{cos}2\text{x}+1}{2}$

$\therefore \text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ y}=\frac{\text{cos}2\text{x}+1}{2}$

$\Rightarrow \text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ y}=\frac{1}{2}+\frac{\text{cos}2\text{x}}{2}$

$\Rightarrow \text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ y}-\frac{1}{2}=\frac{\text{cos}2\text{x}}{2}$

applying transformation $\text{y}-1/{{~}_{2}}\to \text{y}$

$\Rightarrow \text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ y}=\frac{\text{cos}2\text{x}}{2}$

here $\frac{\text{cos}2x}{2}$ have half the period of $\text{cos}x\And $ also half of amplitude.

concepts.title: Transformations done to draw the graph of y=f(x+a) or y=f(x-a) from the known graph of y=f(x)
concepts.conceptBody:
Transformations done to draw the graph of y=f(x+a) or y=f(x-a) from the known graph of y=f(x)

To draw the graph of $y=f\left( x\pm a \right)$ from $y=f\left( x \right)$

(a) To draw $y=f\left( x+a \right)$ from $y=f\left( x \right)$ shift the graph of $f\left( x \right)$ in 'a' units to left.

(b) To draw $y=f\left( x-a \right)$ from $y=f\left( x \right)$ shift the graph of $f\left( x \right)$ by ' $a$ ' units to right.

Q) Plot 
(a) $y=\left| x+2 \right|$ (b) $y=\text{sin}\left( x-\frac{z}{4} \right)$ (c) $y={{4.2}^{\text{x}}}$ 

Solution : (a) for $y=\left| x+2 \right|$

by putting $\text{x}\to \text{x}+2$

$y=\left| x \right|$ 
 

(b) $y=\text{sin}\left( x-\frac{\pi }{4} \right)$

here we will draw the above graph from $\text{y}=\text{sinx}$ putting $\text{x}$ for $\text{x}-\pi /4$ 
 

(c) $y={{4.2}^{\text{x}}}$

or we can write this as

$\text{y}={{2}^{2}}\cdot {{2}^{\text{x}}}={{2}^{\text{x}+2}}$

we know the curve of ${{2}^{x}}$, so

concepts.title: Transformations done to draw the graph of y=f(-x) from the known graph of y=f(x)
concepts.conceptBody:
Transformations done to draw the graph of y=f(-x) from the known graph of y=f(x)

To plot the curve of $y=f\left( -x \right)$ from $y=f\left( x \right)$

  1. Draw the graph $\text{y}=\text{f}\left( \text{x} \right)$
  2. Then take the mirror image of $y=f\left( x \right)$ in $y$-axis or we can say, turn the graph of $f\left( x \right)$ by 180 about y-axis.

Q) Draw the graph of the following : 
(a) $\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ y}={{\text{e}}^{-\text{x}}}$ (b) $y=\text{log}\left( -\text{x} \right)$ 

Solution : 

(a) We will go stepwise for solving these curves.

Step 1 : Draw the graph of $y=f\left( x \right)$

so here putting $-\text{x}$ as $\text{x}$

$\Rightarrow \text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ y}={{\text{e}}^{\text{x}}}$ 
 

(b) $y=\text{log}\left( -x \right)$

$-\text{x}\to \text{x}$

drawing the graph of $y=\text{log}x$

concepts.title: Transformations done to draw the graph of y=-f(x) from the known graph of y=f(x)
concepts.conceptBody:
Transformations done to draw the graph of y=-f(x) from the known graph of y=f(x)

To draw the graph of $y=-f\left( x \right)$ from $y=f\left( x \right)$

Step 1 : Draw the graph of $y=f\left( x \right)$

Step 2 : Then take the mirror image of $y=f\left( x \right)$ in $x$-axis.

Q) Plot the graph of the following curve : 
(a) $\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ y}=-{{\text{e}}^{\text{x}}}$ (b) $y=\text{log}\left( \frac{1}{x} \right)$ 

Solution : 

(a) $\text{y}=-{{\text{e}}^{\text{x}}}$

we know the curve of $y={{e}^{x}}$ 
 

(b) $y=\text{log}\left( \frac{1}{\text{x}} \right)$

this can be written as $\text{y}=\text{log}{{\text{x}}^{-1}}$

or $\text{y}=-\text{logx}\{$ by $\text{log}$ properties $\}$

now we have $\text{y}=-\text{logx}$

concepts.title: Transformations done to draw the graph of y= f(|x|) from the known graph of y=f(x)
concepts.conceptBody:
Transformations done to draw the graph of y= f(|x|) from the known graph of y=f(x)

To plot $y=f\left( \left| x \right| \right)$ from $y=f\left( x \right)$

Step 1 : plot $\text{y}=\text{f}\left( \text{x} \right)$ curve

Step 2 : Remove the left portion of the graph

Step 3 : Take the reflection of right portion in y-axis (including right part also)

Q) Plot the curves of the following : 
(a) $y=\text{log}\left| x \right|$ (b) $y={{x}^{2}}-2\left| x \right|+3$ 
(c) $y=\frac{1}{\left| x \right|+1}$ 

Solution : 

(a) given $\text{y}=\text{log}\left| \text{x} \right|$

Step 1 : draw $\text{y}=\text{f}\left( \text{x} \right)$

Step 2 : remove left part already there is no left part here 
 

(b) $y={{x}^{2}}-2\left| x \right|+3$

This is transformation of $\left| \text{x} \right|\to \text{x}$

Step 1 : putting $\left| \text{x} \right|\to \text{x}$

$\therefore \text{y}={{\text{x}}^{2}}=2\text{x}+3$ drawing the curve

Step 2 : Remove the left part

Step 3 : taking reflection

$y={{x}^{2}}-2x+3$

(c) $\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ y}=\frac{1}{\left| \text{x} \right|+1}$

This one includes 2 transformations

  1. $\left| \text{x} \right|\to \text{x }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }\Rightarrow \text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ y}=\frac{1}{\text{x}+1}$
  2. $x+1\to x\Rightarrow y=\frac{1}{x}$

 

So first drawing the curve $y=\frac{1}{x}$
 

Note : The order of applying the transformations is very important, otherwise we will get wrong answer.

concepts.title: Transformations done to draw the graph of y=|f(x)| from the known curve of y=f(x)
concepts.conceptBody:
Transformations done to draw the graph of y=|f(x)| from the known curve of y=f(x)

To plot $y=\left| f\left( x \right) \right|$ from the curve of $y=f\left( x \right)$

Step 1 : Draw y = f $\left( \text{x} \right)$

Step 2 : Take mirror image of portion below x-axis in $\text{x}$-axis. (removing the lower portion).

Q) Plot the graph of the following curves 

(a) $y=\left| \text{log}x \right|$ (b) $y=\left| {{x}^{2}}-3x+2 \right|$ 

Solution : 

(a) given $\text{y}=\left| \text{f}\left( \text{x} \right) \right|=\left| \text{logx} \right|$

where $f\left( x \right)=\text{log}x$

(b) $y=\left| {{x}^{2}}-3x+2 \right|$

$\Rightarrow \text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ y}=\text{I}\left( \text{x}-1 \right)\left( \text{x}-2 \right)$

applying transformation $\left| \text{f}\left( \text{x} \right) \right|\to \text{f}\left( \text{x} \right)$

$\therefore \text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }f\left( x \right)=\left( x-1 \right)\left( x-2 \right)$

concepts.title: Transformations done to draw the graph of |y|=f(x) from the known curve of y=f(x)
concepts.conceptBody:
Transformations done to draw the graph of |y|=f(x) from the known curve of y=f(x)

To plot the graph of $\left| y \right|=f\left( x \right)$ from $y=f\left( x \right)$

Step 1 : draw $\text{y}=\text{f}\left( \text{x} \right)$

Step 2 : Remove the lower portion i.e. below x-axis.

Step 3 : Take mirror image of upper part in lower part, keeping the upper part also.

Q) Draw the graph of the following : 

(a) $\left| \text{y} \right|=\left( \text{x}-2 \right)\left( \text{x}-3 \right)$ 

(b) $\left| y \right|=\text{log}x$ 

Solution : 

(a) given $\left| \text{y} \right|=\left( \text{x}-2 \right)\left( \text{x}-3 \right)$

applying the 3 steps

(b) $\left| y \right|=\text{log}x$

concepts.title: Transformations done to plot the graph of x=f(y) from the known curve of y=f(x)
concepts.conceptBody:
Transformations done to plot the graph of x=f(y) from the known curve of y=f(x)

To plot $\text{x}=\text{f}\left( \text{y} \right)$ from $\text{y}=\text{f}\left( \text{x} \right)$

Step 1 : Draw $\text{y}=\text{f}\left( \text{x} \right)$

Step 2 : Take reflection in line $\text{y}=\text{x}$, also called reflection about origin.

Q) Plot the graph of the following : 
(a) $x=\text{log}y$ (b) $x=\left| y \right|$ 

Solution : 

(a) now here $\text{x}\And \text{y}$ are interchanged original function was $\text{y}=\text{logx}$ 
 

(b) we have $\text{x}=\left| \text{y} \right|$ & we know the graph of $\text{y}=\left| \text{x} \right|$, which we get by replacing $\text{x}$ by

$\text{y}\And \text{y}$ by $\text{x}$.

concepts.title: Transformations done to plot the graph of x=|f(y)| from the known curve of x=f(y)
concepts.conceptBody:
Transformations done to plot the graph of x=|f(y)| from the known curve of x=f(y)

To plot $\text{x}=\left| \text{f}\left( \text{y} \right) \right|$ from the graph $\text{x}=\text{f}\left( \text{y} \right)$

Step 1 : Draw the graph of $\text{x}=\text{f}\left( \text{y} \right)$, using transformation 8 .

Step 1 : Take reflection of Left portion in y axis.

Q) Plot the graph of the following : 

(a) $\text{x}=\left| \text{logy} \right|$

Solution : 

Firstly drawing the graph of $\text{x}=\text{logy}$ from $\text{y}=\text{logx}$ 
 

now applying step 2 for modulus function

Note : This transformation is not valid for $g\left( x \right)=\left| f\left( y \right) \right|$ i.e. on L.H.S. only $x$ should be there

& no other function.

concepts.title: Transformations done to plot the graph of y=[f(x)] from the known curve of y=f(x)
concepts.conceptBody:
Transformations done to plot the graph of y=[f(x)] from the known curve of y=f(x)

To plot the graph of $y=\left[ f\left( x \right) \right]$ from $y=f\left( x \right)$

Step 1: Draw y = f (x)

Step 2 : Draw horizontal lines after every unit distance i.e. $\text{y}\in \text{k}$, where $\text{k}$ integers.

Step 3 : From the point of intersection (as obtained from step 2), draw vertical lines.

Step 4 : From the intersection points draw horizontal lines upto the nearest vertical line

(towards right). The line drawn should be below the curve for that region.

We will understand the steps with the help of examples.

Draw the graph of following curves : 

(i) $\text{y}=\left[ \text{x} \right]$ (ii) $\text{y}=\left[ {{\text{x}}^{3}} \right]$ (iii) $\text{y}=\left[ 2\text{sinx} \right]$

Solution : 

(a) We will go stepwise, so as to understand the procedure.

we have $\text{y}=\left[ \text{x} \right]$

(b) $\text{y}=\left[ {{\text{x}}^{2}} \right]$

 

(c) $\text{y}=\left[ 2\text{sinx} \right]$

 

Drawing first $2\text{sinx}$, which is almost same curve as $\text{sinx}$ but has an amplitude of

2 rather than 1.

concepts.title: Transformations done to plot the graph of y=f([x]) from the known curve of y=f(x)
concepts.conceptBody:
Transformations done to plot the graph of y=f([x]) from the known curve of y=f(x)

To plot $y=f\left( \left[ x \right] \right)$ from the graph of $y=f\left( x \right)$

Step 1 : Draw $\text{y}=\text{f}\left( \text{x} \right)$

Step 2 : Draw vertical lines on every integral point of $\text{x}$ i.e. $\text{x}=\text{k}$ where $\text{k}\in \text{I}$ (integers)

Step 3 : Draw horizontal lines from point of intersection to the nearest right vertical line.

Plot the following curves : 

(a) $\text{y}={{\text{e}}^{\left[ \text{x} \right]}}$ (b) $y=\text{sin}\left[ x \right]$

Solution : 

(a) given $\text{y}={{\text{e}}^{\left[ \text{x} \right]}}$

we know the graph of $y={{e}^{x}}$

Step 1

(b) $y=\text{sin}\left[ \text{x} \right]$

here we know the graph of $y=\text{sin}x$ 
 

In step 2 we have marked lines for $\text{x}=\text{k}$ (where $\text{k}\in $ integers)

Note here that $\pi $ has a value 3.14 (approx.), so now we can understand that we will get

point $\text{x}=1,2,3$ between 0 and $\pi $.

Note here that the figure in step 2 has parts above and below x-axis in $\left( 3,4 \right)$ but in final

graph the graph between $\left( 3,4 \right)$ is above $\text{x}$ axis.

This is so because, for $3\le \text{x}<4,\left[ \text{x} \right]=3$ only, so the value will be $\text{sin}3$.

Also do not get confuse in values $\text{sin}1,\text{sin}2\And \text{sin}3$.

For $1\le \text{x}<2;\text{sin}\left[ \text{x} \right]=\text{sin}1\sim .84$

$2\le \text{x}<3;\text{sin}\left[ \text{x} \right]=\text{sin}2\sim .909$

$3\le \text{x}<4;\text{sin}\left[ \text{x} \right]=\text{sin}3\sim .14$

$\And 1,2\And 3$ are in radians.

concepts.title: Transformations done to plot the graph of [y]=f(x) from the known curve of y=f(x)
concepts.conceptBody:
Transformations done to plot the graph of [y]=f(x) from the known curve of y=f(x)

To plot $\left[ y \right]=f\left( x \right)$, from $y=f\left( x \right)$

Step 1 : Draw $\text{y}=\text{f}\left( \text{x} \right)$

Step 2 : Draw horizontal lines at a unit distance i.e. $y=k$ ( $\text{k}$ belongs to set of integers)

Step 3 : Draw vertical lines from the point of intersection up till next upper horizontal

line Include only the lower point.

Plot the following graph : 

(a) $\left[ y \right]={{x}^{3}}$ 

(b) $\left[ y \right]={{x}^{2}}-2$ 

Solution : 

(a) given $\left[ y \right]={{x}^{3}}$, we known the graph of $y={{x}^{3}}$

 

Step $1\And 2$

(b) $\left[ \text{y} \right]={{\text{x}}^{2}}-2$

This one includes two transformations:

  1. $\left[ \text{y} \right]\to \text{y}\Rightarrow \text{y}={{\text{x}}^{2}}-2$
  2. $\text{y}+2\to 2\Rightarrow \text{y}={{\text{x}}^{2}}$

 

Drawing first $\text{y}={{\text{x}}^{2}}$ 
So, you can note here that upper points on the

vertical lines are not included.

concepts.title: Transformations done to plot the graph of x=[f(y)] from the known curve of x=f(y)
concepts.conceptBody:
Transformations done to plot the graph of x=[f(y)] from the known curve of x=f(y)

To plot $\text{x}=\left[ \text{f}\left( \text{y} \right) \right]$ from $\text{x}=\text{f}\left( \text{y} \right)$

Step 1 : draw $\text{x}=\text{f}\left( \text{y} \right)$ by using transformation 8 .

Step 2 : draw vertical lines at a unit distance

i.e. $\text{x}=\text{k}(\text{k}\in $ integers $)$

Step 3 : draw vertical lines from the point of intersection uptil the above intersection point.

Q) Draw the graph of $\text{x}=\left[ \sqrt{\text{y}} \right]$ 

Solution : 

Given $x=\left[ \sqrt{\text{y}} \right]$

first we will draw the graph of $\text{x}=\sqrt{\text{y}}$

for $\text{x}=\sqrt{\text{y}},\text{y}\ge 0$ (as it is a property of under root function)

squaring $\Rightarrow \text{x}\ge 0$

$\Rightarrow {{\text{x}}^{2}}=\text{y}$

 

Here we will draw in the region where $x\ge 0$ as stated by (1)

Note : This transformation is not valid for functions of form $g\left( x \right)=\left[ f\left( y \right) \right]$

concepts.title: Transformations done to plot the graph of y=f({x}) from the known curve of y=f(x)
concepts.conceptBody:
Transformations done to plot the graph of y=f({x}) from the known curve of y=f(x)

Plot the graph of $y=f\left( \left\{ x \right\} \right)$ from $y=f\left( x \right)$

Step 1 : draw the graph of $y=f\left( x \right)$ in the interval $\left[ 0,1 \right]$

Step 2 : Repeat the same graph as in step 1, with a period of 1 .

Q) Plot the graph of the following : 

(a) $y=\text{sin}\left( x-\left[ x \right] \right)$ (b) $y=\frac{{{2}^{\text{x}}}}{{{2}^{\left[ \text{x} \right]}}}$ 

Solution : 

(a) given $\text{y}=\text{sin}\left( \text{x}-\left[ \text{x} \right] \right)$

$\And $ we know that $\text{x}-\left[ \text{x} \right]=\left\{ \text{x} \right\}$

$\therefore \text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }$ we have to draw $\text{y}=\text{sinx}$ in $\left[ 0,1 \right]$

(b) $\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ y}=\frac{{{2}^{\text{x}}}}{{{2}^{\left[ \text{x} \right]}}}$

this could be written as $y={{2}^{x-\left[ x \right]}}\Rightarrow y={{2}^{\left\{ x \right\}}}$

for this we will draw $y={{a}^{x}}$ with $a>0$

 

For $\left\{ \text{x} \right\}$ graph we will only check for the output between 0 and 1 .

concepts.title: Transformations done to plot the graph of y={f(x)} from the known curve of y=f(x)
concepts.conceptBody:
Transformations done to plot the graph of y={f(x)} from the known curve of y=f(x)

To draw the graph of $y=\left\{ f\left( x \right) \right\}$ from $y=f\left( x \right)$

Step 1 : Draw the graph of $y=f\left( x \right)$

Step 2 : Transfer the graph between the interval

$y=0\And y=1$

Q) Plot the graph of the following : 

(a) $y=\left\{ {{\text{e}}^{\text{x}-1}} \right\}$ (b) $y=\left\{ 2\text{sin}x \right\}$

Solution : 

(a) given that $y=\left\{ {{e}^{x-1}} \right\}$

$\left\{ \text{f}\left( \text{x} \right) \right\}\to \text{f}\left( \text{x} \right)\Rightarrow \text{y}={{\text{e}}^{\text{x}-1}}$

$\text{x}-1\to \text{x }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }\Rightarrow \text{y}={{\text{e}}^{\text{x}}}$ 

(b) $y=\left\{ 2\text{sin}x \right\}$

We know the graph of $y=2\text{sin}x$, as we have done that earlier also.

concepts.title: Transformations required to draw the graph of {y}=f(x) from the known curve of y=f(x)
concepts.conceptBody:
Transformations required to draw the graph of {y}=f(x) from the known curve of y=f(x)

$\text{y}=\text{f}\left( \text{x} \right)\to \left\{ \text{y} \right\}=\text{f}\left( \text{x} \right)$

To draw $\left\{ \text{y} \right\}=\text{f}\left( \text{x} \right)$. Draw the graph of $\text{y}=\text{f}\left( \text{x} \right)$ then retain the graph of $\text{y}=\text{f}\left( \text{x} \right)$

which lies between $\text{y}\in \left[ 0,1 \right)$ and neglect the graph for other values. Also repeat this graph in

the same interval for $\text{x}$, but for all intervals $y\in \left[ n,n+1 \right)$.

Q) Plot the graph of $\left\{ y \right\}={{e}^{-x}}$ 

Solution : 

(i) \(\begin{align} & \text{ y } \} \text{ = }{{\text{e}}^{-x}}\leftarrow y={{e}^{-x}} \\ & y=\{y\} \\ \end{align}\)

concepts.title: Transformations required to plot the graph of x={f(y)} from the known curve of x=f(y)
concepts.conceptBody:
Transformation 16 

$x=\text{f}\left( y \right)\to \,\,x=\{\text{f}\,\text{(y)}\}$

To draw $x=\left\{ f\left( y \right) \right\}$. Draw $x=f\left( y \right)$. Draw vertical lines corresponding to integral

values of $x$. Transfer the graph between two consecutive vertical lines to the region lying

between $\text{x}=0\And \text{x}=1$. Don't include the points lying on $\text{x}=1$.

Q) Plot (i) $\text{x}=\left\{ 2\text{y} \right\}$ 

Solution : $x=\left\{ 2y \right\}\overleftarrow{f\left( y \right)=f\left\{ y \right\}}x=2y$ 

 

Graph of $\text{x}=2\text{y}$ Graph of $\text{x}=\left\{ 2\text{y} \right\}$

Note : This transformation is not valid for drawing $g\left( x \right)=\left\{ f\left( y \right) \right\}$ from $g\left( x \right)=f\left( y \right)$

concepts.title: Transformations required to plot the graph of y=Signum(f(x)) from the known curve of y=f(x)
concepts.conceptBody:
Transformations required to plot the graph of y=Signum(f(x)) from the known curve of y=f(x)

$\text{y}=\text{f}\left( \text{x} \right)\text{y}=\text{sgn}\left( \text{f}\left( \text{x} \right) \right)$

To Draw $y=\text{sgn}\left( \text{f}\left( \text{x} \right) \right)$. Draw $\text{y}=\text{f}\left( \text{x} \right)$. Then draw $\text{y}=1$ for which $\text{f}\left( \text{x} \right)>0$ and

$\text{y}=-1$ for which $\text{f}\left( \text{x} \right)<0$ and $\text{y}=0$ for which $\text{f}\left( \text{x} \right)=0$.

Q) Plot the graph of $\text{y}=\text{sgn}\left( \text{logx} \right)$ 

Solution :
